title: Text2CAD: Generating Sequential CAD Models from Beginner-to-Expert Level Text Prompts
abstract: We propose Text2CAD, the first AI framework for generating text-to-parametric CAD models using designer-friendly instructions for all skill levels. We introduce a data annotation pipeline producing approximately 170,000 models with 660,000 text annotations, ranging from abstract CAD descriptions to detailed specifications. The framework employs an end-to-end transformer-based auto-regressive network to convert text inputs into parametric CAD models.
method_summary: End-to-end transformer-based autoregressive network that converts multi-level text prompts into parametric CAD command sequences. Uses a data annotation pipeline with Mistral and LLaVA-NeXT to create 170K models with 660K text annotations at varying detail levels.

title: CAD-Coder: Text-to-CAD Generation with Chain-of-Thought and Geometric Reward
abstract: We introduce CAD-Coder, a novel framework that reformulates text-to-CAD as the generation of CadQuery scripts, a Python-based parametric CAD language. This representation enables direct geometric validation and integrates with existing LLMs. We employ a two-stage pipeline combining supervised fine-tuning on paired text-CadQuery data with reinforcement learning using Group Reward Policy Optimization, guided by rewards measuring both geometric fidelity and format correctness. We introduce a chain-of-thought planning process and construct a dataset comprising 110K text-CadQuery-3D model triplets and 1.5K CoT samples.
method_summary: Two-stage pipeline: SFT on text-CadQuery pairs followed by RL with GRPO using geometric fidelity and format rewards. Introduces chain-of-thought planning for structured CAD reasoning. Generates CadQuery Python scripts directly.

title: Zero-to-CAD: Agentic Synthesis of Interpretable CAD Programs at Million-Scale Without Real Data
abstract: We introduce Zero-to-CAD, a framework that synthesizes executable CAD sequences by embedding a large language model within a feedback-driven CAD environment to iteratively generate and validate code. It produces approximately one million executable CAD sequences and a curated subset of 100K high-quality models. Fine-tuning a vision-language model on synthetic data to reconstruct editable CAD programs from images outperforms strong baselines including GPT-5.2.
method_summary: Agentic search framework embedding LLM in feedback-driven CAD environment for iterative code generation and validation. Produces 1M synthetic CAD sequences without real data. Downstream VLM fine-tuning for image-to-CAD reconstruction.

title: FutureCAD: Towards High-Fidelity CAD Generation via LLM-Driven Program Generation and Text-Based B-Rep Primitive Grounding
abstract: We present FutureCAD, a framework bridging parametric CAD modeling and boundary representation synthesis. It leverages LLMs and a B-Rep grounding transformer (BRepGround) to generate executable CadQuery scripts, enabling natural language specification of geometric selections. We apply supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning on a new real-world CAD dataset.
method_summary: LLM generates CadQuery scripts with text-based geometric selection queries, grounded by BRepGround transformer to actual B-Rep primitives. Combines SFT + RL training on real-world CAD data for high-fidelity generation.

title: Seek-CAD: A Self-refined Generative Modeling for 3D Parametric CAD Using Local Inference via DeepSeek
abstract: Seek-CAD is the pioneer exploration of locally deployed open-source inference LLM DeepSeek-R1 for CAD parametric model generation with a training-free methodology. It introduces a self-refinement mechanism combining visual feedback and Chain-of-Thought reasoning from a Vision Language Model to iteratively improve generated designs. We present a new 3D CAD dataset organized around the SSR paradigm covering diverse CAD commands.
method_summary: Training-free approach using locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 with self-refinement via visual feedback and VLM-based CoT reasoning. Iteratively improves CAD designs without fine-tuning. Introduces SSR-paradigm dataset.

title: ReCAD: Reinforcement Learning Enhanced Parametric CAD Model Generation with Vision-Language Models
abstract: We present ReCAD, an RL framework that bootstraps pretrained large models to generate precise parametric CAD models from multimodal inputs. We fine-tune vision-language models using parameterized code for accurate textual supervision. A novel RL strategy incorporates parameterized code as guidance with hierarchical primitive learning under a unified reward function balancing geometric accuracy and semantic fidelity.
method_summary: RL framework bootstrapping pretrained VLMs for parametric CAD generation. Uses parameterized code as supervision, hierarchical primitive learning, and unified reward balancing geometric accuracy with semantic fidelity. Achieves major CD reduction on image-to-CAD.

title: cadrille: Multi-modal CAD Reconstruction with Online Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We present cadrille, a multimodal CAD reconstruction model that processes point clouds, images, and text simultaneously. Following LLM training paradigms, we employ supervised fine-tuning on procedurally generated data followed by reinforcement learning using online feedback. Online RL algorithms such as GRPO outperform offline alternatives. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results on three datasets including a real-world benchmark.
method_summary: Multi-modal VLM for CAD reconstruction from point clouds, images, and text. SFT on procedural data + online RL (GRPO) fine-tuning. First exploration of RL fine-tuning for CAD tasks. Generates executable CadQuery Python code.

title: CAD-RL: From Intent to Execution: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for Precise CAD Code Generation
abstract: We introduce CAD-RL, which leverages LLMs to automate parametric 3D modeling through multimodal Chain-of-Thought guided reinforcement learning post training. The framework combines reasoning-based initialization with goal-driven learning using three reward signals: executability, geometric accuracy, and external evaluation. We implement Trust Region Stretch, Precision Token Loss, and Overlong Filtering. We release ExeCAD, a dataset of 16,540 real-world CAD examples.
method_summary: Multimodal CoT-guided RL post-training for CAD code generation. Three-reward system (executability, geometric accuracy, external evaluation). Novel techniques: Trust Region Stretch for exploration, Precision Token Loss, Overlong Filtering. Releases ExeCAD dataset (16.5K).

title: RLCAD: Reinforcement Learning Training Gym for Revolution Involved CAD Command Sequence Generation
abstract: We introduce a reinforcement learning environment built on a CAD geometric engine for generating command sequences from boundary representation geometry. Given an input B-Rep geometry, the policy network generates actions processed through the gym to produce CAD geometry with rewards from geometric differences. The approach extends beyond prior work by supporting revolution operations alongside sketching, Boolean, and extrusion operations.
method_summary: RL training gymnasium built on CAD geometric engine. Policy network generates CAD commands from B-Rep input, receives geometric difference rewards. Extends to revolution operations beyond standard sketch-extrude.

title: Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: This survey examines how LLMs have seen rapid advancements and explores their integration with CAD systems. It outlines CAD's industrial importance, reviews LLM foundations including both proprietary and open-source models, and presents a taxonomy of six key application areas where LLMs impact CAD workflows. The authors also propose future research directions.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of LLM+CAD integration covering six application areas. Reviews proprietary and open-source LLM foundations and proposes future research directions for CAD technology through LLM integration.

title: CAD-Llama: Leveraging Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design Parametric 3D Model Generation
abstract: We investigate using LLMs to generate parametric sequences for CAD models. LLMs neither encounter parametric sequences during pretraining nor possess direct awareness of 3D structures. We introduce CAD-Llama with a hierarchical annotation pipeline and Structured Parametric CAD Code (SPCC) format. The framework combines adaptive pretraining on SPCC with instruction tuning aligned to CAD-specific guidelines.
method_summary: Hierarchical annotation pipeline + Structured Parametric CAD Code (SPCC) format to bridge LLM pretraining gap. Adaptive pretraining on SPCC + instruction tuning for CAD-specific spatial awareness.

title: CADCodeVerify: Generating CAD Code with Vision-Language Models for 3D Designs
abstract: We introduce CADCodeVerify, a method for iteratively verifying and improving 3D objects generated from CAD code. The approach uses VLMs to generate and answer validation questions to verify the generated object and prompt the VLM to correct deviations. We present CADPrompt, a benchmark with 200 natural language prompts paired with expert-annotated CAD scripting code.
method_summary: Iterative verification loop using VLMs to generate validation questions, check generated CAD objects, and correct deviations. Introduces CADPrompt benchmark (200 prompts with expert annotations).

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: We present CAD-GPT which introduces a 3D Modeling Spatial Mechanism that maps spatial positions and sketch plane rotations into linguistic feature space using a specialized unfolding mechanism, while discretizing 2D sketch coordinates for precise spatial determination. The method accepts either images or text as input.
method_summary: 3D Modeling Spatial Mechanism mapping spatial positions and rotations into linguistic feature space. Unfolding mechanism + coordinate discretization for precise spatial reasoning in multimodal LLMs.

title: ProCAD: Clarify Before You Draw: Proactive Agents for Robust Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: We introduce ProCAD, a proactive framework addressing specification issues before code synthesis. It combines a clarifying agent that audits prompts and poses targeted questions with a CAD coding agent translating specifications into executable CadQuery programs. ProCAD surpasses frontier models including Claude Sonnet 4.5, achieving 79.9% reduction in mean Chamfer distance.
method_summary: Proactive two-agent framework: clarifying agent audits prompts for ambiguity/conflicts before CAD coding agent generates CadQuery code. Agentic SFT on clarification trajectories. Addresses robustness to ambiguous inputs.

title: ToolCAD: Exploring Tool-Using Large Language Models in Text-to-CAD Generation with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We introduce ToolCAD, which deploys LLMs as tool-using agents for text-to-CAD generation. We develop an interactive CAD modeling environment for generating reasoning trajectories with hybrid feedback and human guidance. An end-to-end post-training strategy enables LLM agents to develop refined CAD Modeling Chain of Thought through online curriculum reinforcement learning.
method_summary: LLM as tool-using agent interacting with CAD engine. Interactive environment generates reasoning trajectories with hybrid feedback. Online curriculum RL for developing CAD Modeling CoT. Bridges open-source LLMs to proprietary model performance.

title: Text-to-CadQuery: A New Paradigm for CAD Generation with Scalable Large Model Capabilities
abstract: We propose direct generation of CadQuery code from natural language, leveraging pretrained LLMs' existing strengths in Python and spatial reasoning. We augmented the Text2CAD dataset with 170,000 CadQuery annotations and fine-tuned six open-source models. The best model achieves a top-1 exact match of 69.3% and reduces Chamfer Distance by 48.6%.
method_summary: Direct CadQuery code generation from text leveraging LLM Python proficiency. Augments Text2CAD with 170K CadQuery annotations. Fine-tunes multiple open-source LLMs showing consistent scaling improvements.

title: Learning From Design Procedure To Generate CAD Programs for Data Augmentation
abstract: We propose a data augmentation paradigm that prompts an LLM to generate CAD programs conditioned on a reference surface program and a modeling procedure, inspired by industrial design practices. By varying reference surfaces using organic shapes, the approach enriches geometric distribution and introduces spline-based curvature features.
method_summary: LLM-based data augmentation for CAD: generates programs conditioned on reference surfaces + modeling procedures. Introduces organic shapes and spline-based features to enrich geometric diversity beyond standard datasets.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: We propose CadVLM, an end-to-end vision language model for CAD generation. Our approach adapts pre-trained foundation models to manipulate engineering sketches effectively, integrating sketch primitives and images. This represents the first instance of a multimodal LLM being successfully applied to parametric CAD generation.
method_summary: First multimodal LLM applied to parametric CAD sketch generation. Adapts pre-trained VLMs to integrate sketch primitives and images for autocompletion, autoconstraint, and image-conditional generation tasks.

title: PLLM: Pseudo-Labeling Large Language Models for CAD Program Synthesis
abstract: We introduce PLLM, a self-training framework for CAD program synthesis from unlabeled 3D shapes. Given a pre-trained CAD-capable LLM and a shape dataset, PLLM iteratively samples candidate programs, selects high-fidelity executions, and augments programs to construct synthetic program-shape pairs for fine-tuning.
method_summary: Self-training framework for CAD program synthesis without paired data. Iterative sampling, selection of high-fidelity executions, and augmentation to create synthetic training pairs from unlabeled 3D shapes.

title: CADEvolve: Creating Realistic CAD via Program Evolution
abstract: We present CADEvolve, an evolution-based pipeline that grows CAD programs from simple primitives using VLM-guided edits and validations. The approach produces 8,000 complex parts as executable CadQuery generators, yielding 1.3 million scripts paired with rendered geometry after post-processing. A fine-tuned VLM achieves state-of-the-art on Image2CAD tasks.
method_summary: Evolution-based pipeline growing CAD programs from primitives via VLM-guided edits and validation. Produces 1.3M CadQuery scripts. Addresses complexity gap in public CAD corpora by generating industrial-grade parts.

title: CAD-Tokenizer: Towards Text-based CAD Prototyping via Modality-Specific Tokenization
abstract: We propose CAD-Tokenizer, which uses a sequence-based VQ-VAE with primitive-level pooling and constrained decoding to produce compact, structure-aware representations. Standard LLM tokenizers fail to capture primitive-level CAD semantics. Applied to unified text-to-CAD generation and editing.
method_summary: Modality-specific tokenization for CAD using VQ-VAE with primitive-level pooling. Captures CAD semantics better than standard LLM tokenizers. Enables unified text-to-CAD generation and editing.

title: OpenECAD: An Efficient Visual Language Model for Editable 3D-CAD Design
abstract: We present OpenECAD, fine-tuned visual language models (0.55B to 3.1B parameters) that transform 3D design images into editable CAD outputs with highly structured 2D sketches and 3D construction commands. Uses dependency-based sketch generation and specialized datasets from public CAD repositories.
method_summary: Efficient VLMs (0.55B-3.1B) fine-tuned for image-to-editable-CAD. Dependency-based sketch generation producing structured 2D sketches and 3D commands compatible with CAD APIs.

title: From Idea to CAD: A Language Model-Driven Multi-Agent System for Collaborative Design
abstract: We present a VLM-based Multi Agent System with access to parametric CAD tooling and tool documentation. Combining agents for requirements engineering, CAD engineering, and vision-based quality assurance, a model is generated automatically from sketches and/or textual descriptions.
method_summary: Multi-agent system mirroring engineering team structure: requirements agent, CAD agent, QA agent. VLM-based with parametric CAD tool access. Iterative refinement with user input.

title: CADDesigner: Conceptual Design of CAD Models Based on General-Purpose Agent
abstract: We present an LLM-powered agent that accepts textual and sketch inputs, refines design requirements through interactive dialogue, and generates CAD code with iterative visual feedback. Built on the Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm with a structured knowledge base for continuous improvement.
method_summary: LLM agent with interactive dialogue for requirement refinement. Context-Independent Imperative Paradigm (CIP) for code generation. Iterative visual feedback loop with structured knowledge base.

title: TCADGen: Automated CAD Modeling Sequence Generation from Text Descriptions via Transformer-Based Large Language Models
abstract: We propose a language-guided framework combining LLMs with computer-automated design. Key contributions include a semi-automated data annotation pipeline, a Transformer-based CAD generator (TCADGen) using dual-channel feature aggregation, and CADLLM that refines generated sequences using confidence scores.
method_summary: Transformer-based CAD generator with dual-channel feature aggregation. Semi-automated annotation pipeline using LLMs+VLMs. CADLLM refinement using confidence scores for industrial workflow automation.

title: Generative AI for CAD Automation: Leveraging Large Language Models for 3D Modelling
abstract: We explore how LLMs can streamline CAD workflows by integrating with FreeCAD. LLMs generate initial CAD scripts from natural language descriptions, which are then executed and refined iteratively based on error feedback. Testing across designs of varying complexity shows the approach works well for simpler models but encounters difficulties with highly constrained designs.
method_summary: LLM integration with FreeCAD for iterative script generation and error-feedback refinement. Empirical study across varying complexity levels identifying limitations with constrained designs.

title: Query2CAD: Generating CAD models using natural language queries
abstract: We introduce Query2CAD, a framework leveraging LLMs to generate CAD designs from natural language without supervised training data. Uses LLM as both generator and refiner with BLIP2 feedback and human-in-the-loop corrections. GPT-4 Turbo achieves 53.6% success rate on first attempt with 23.1% improvement through refinements.
method_summary: Training-free LLM-based CAD generation with self-refinement loop. BLIP2 visual feedback + human-in-the-loop corrections. Demonstrates diminishing returns after first refinement iteration.

title: CAD-Judge: Toward Efficient Morphological Grading and Verification for Text-to-CAD Generation
abstract: We propose CAD-Judge, a novel verifiable reward system for efficient CAD preference grading and grammatical validation. Uses Compiler-as-a-Judge Module for fast reward signals and Compiler-as-a-Review Module for verification. Employs prospect theory optimization and agentic generation.
method_summary: Verifiable reward system for CAD generation evaluation. Compiler-as-a-Judge for fast rewards + Compiler-as-a-Review for verification. Prospect theory optimization for preference grading.

title: Human-in-the-Loop: Quantitative Evaluation of 3D Models Generation by Large Language Models
abstract: We introduce a human-in-the-loop framework for quantitative evaluation of LLM-generated 3D models. We propose metrics including volumetric accuracy, surface alignment, dimensional fidelity, and topological intricacy. Using an L-bracket case study, we compare LLM performance across four input modalities.
method_summary: Quantitative evaluation framework for LLM-generated CAD models. Metrics: volumetric accuracy, surface alignment, dimensional fidelity, topological intricacy. Compares four input modalities showing code-level prompts achieve perfect reconstruction.
input_modality: multiple (2D views, isometric, structure tree, code)

title: CAD-Coder (MIT): An Open-Source Vision-Language Model for Computer-Aided Design Code Generation
abstract: We introduce CAD-Coder, a fine-tuned VLM that generates editable CAD code in CadQuery Python from visual input. Built on GenCAD-Code dataset with over 163K image-code pairs. Outperforms GPT-4.5 and Qwen2.5-VL-72B, achieving 100% valid syntax rate. Demonstrates generalizability to real-world images.
method_summary: Fine-tuned VLM for image-to-CadQuery code generation. GenCAD-Code dataset (163K image-code pairs). Achieves 100% valid syntax rate, outperforming GPT-4.5 and Qwen2.5-VL-72B.

title: Text2CAD: Text to 3D CAD Generation via Technical Drawings
abstract: We introduce a framework that employs stable diffusion models to automate CAD generation from text. Converts textual specifications into isometric images, then transforms into orthographic views (top, front, side) to reconstruct 3D CAD models while maintaining physical and dimensional consistency.
method_summary: Text -> isometric image (via diffusion) -> orthographic views -> 3D CAD reconstruction pipeline. Uses stable diffusion for intermediate visual representation maintaining engineering consistency.

title: GenCAD-3D: CAD Program Generation using Multimodal Latent Space Alignment and Synthetic Dataset Balancing
abstract: We introduce GenCAD-3D, a multimodal framework using contrastive learning to align latent embeddings between CAD and geometric encoders, combined with latent diffusion models for sequence generation and retrieval. SynthBal, a synthetic data augmentation strategy, balances and expands datasets enhancing complex geometry representation.
method_summary: Contrastive learning aligns CAD and geometric encoder latent spaces. Latent diffusion for sequence generation. SynthBal augmentation strategy for dataset balancing, improving complex geometry performance.

title: Drawing2CAD: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CAD Generation from Vectorized Drawings
abstract: We reframe CAD generation from 2D engineering drawings as a sequence-to-sequence learning problem where vector drawing primitives directly inform parametric CAD operations. Drawing2CAD comprises vector primitive representation, dual-decoder transformer, and soft target distribution loss. We created CAD-VGDrawing paired dataset.
method_summary: Seq2seq learning from vectorized engineering drawings to parametric CAD. Dual-decoder transformer decoupling command type and parameter generation. Soft target distribution loss for parameter flexibility.

title: CADCrafter: Generating Computer-Aided Design Models from Unconstrained Images
abstract: We present CADCrafter, which trains solely on synthetic textureless CAD data while testing on real-world images. A geometry encoder captures diverse geometric features with texture-invariant properties. Since CAD parameter compilation is non-differentiable, we use direct preference optimization with automatic code checker feedback to enforce geometric validity. We collected a real-world dataset of multi-view images paired with CAD command sequences.
method_summary: Image-to-CAD from unconstrained real images. Trains on synthetic textureless data, generalizes to real images via texture-invariant geometry encoder. DPO with code checker feedback for geometric validity.

title: CAD2Program: From 2D CAD Drawings to 3D Parametric Models: A Vision-Language Approach
abstract: We present CAD2Program, a method for converting 2D CAD drawings into 3D parametric models using vision-language techniques. Treats 2D drawings as raster images encoded with a standard Vision Transformer, generating text-based descriptions of 3D parametric models through autoregressive prediction.
method_summary: VLM-based 2D drawing to 3D parametric model conversion. ViT encodes raster drawings, autoregressive text generation for flexible parametric model description. Extends to diverse geometric entities.

title: Image2CADSeq: Computer-Aided Design Sequence and Knowledge Inference from Product Images
abstract: We present a neural network approach that converts product images into CAD operation sequences rather than traditional boundary representation models. CAD sequences offer enhanced flexibility to modify individual steps of model creation. We developed a multi-level evaluation framework and trained on a synthesized dataset.
method_summary: Neural network converting product images to CAD operation sequences (not B-Rep). Multi-level evaluation framework for assessing sequence quality at different granularities.
